title: System tests: cleanup in --format test
body:
Followup to #15673 (--format with newlines). I cobbled up a test
for it, but I was sloppy, so the test had issues that I kept
having to band-aid. This is a cleaner way to handle podman-machine.

...and, another unexpected surprise with podman stats. It
fails under rootless cgroupsv1. We can't sweep it under the
rug via skip_if_ubuntu because tests will then fail on RHEL8.
So, add a similar mechanism for testing podman stats.
    
...plus a non-surprise, the 'search' test flakes. Try minimizing
that by searching only $IMAGE. If quay.io is down, other tests
will certainly fail.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

/hold

The `search` test is [flaking](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4517455476293632/html/sys-podman-fedora-36-aarch64-root-host.log.html). I was afraid of that, because `search` means network which means flakes. Is there any way to call `podman search --format` without actually trying to ping a registry?

> The `search` test is [flaking](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4517455476293632/html/sys-podman-fedora-36-aarch64-root-host.log.html). I was afraid of that, because `search` means network which means flakes. Is there any way to call `podman search --format` without actually trying to ping a registry?

No the code has to search before it can display the results. Maybe use a local registry?
Went with `search $IMAGE`, which should (I assume) hit only `quay.io`, and if `quay.io` is broken, then all tests will fail anyway.
